methods.title: Community energy use per capita
methods.equation: Energy per capita = annual final energy / population
assumptions: Energy inventory and population use the same geographic boundary.

methods.title: Community water use per capita
methods.equation: Water per capita = annual water / population
assumptions: Annual water demand and population use the same service boundary.

methods.title: Municipal waste generation per capita
methods.equation: Waste per capita = annual waste / population
assumptions: Waste inventory and population use the same geographic boundary.

methods.title: Material circularity rate
methods.equation: Circularity = secondary material input / total material input
assumptions: Material flows use compatible lifecycle and geographic boundaries.

methods.title: Resource recovery rate
methods.equation: Recovery = recovered waste / generated waste
assumptions: Recovered and generated material use the same waste-stream scope.

methods.title: Annual community food demand
methods.equation: Food demand = population × unit food demand
assumptions: Unit food demand is a stated scenario assumption.

methods.title: Water-system electricity demand
methods.equation: Electricity = water volume × energy intensity
assumptions: Energy intensity includes the selected supply, treatment, and conveyance stages.

methods.title: Composite urban metabolism intensity
methods.equation: Intensity = weighted normalized energy + water + waste
assumptions: Component indices use a common normalized scale; lower or higher desirability must be declared separately.

methods.title: Community greenhouse-gas inventory
methods.equation: Emissions = electricity + fuel + transport + waste
assumptions: Sector inventories use compatible accounting methods and boundaries.

methods.title: Community emissions per capita
methods.equation: Emissions per capita = annual emissions / population
assumptions: Emissions and population use the same geographic boundary.

methods.title: Constant annual emissions-reduction trajectory
methods.equation: Annual reduction = (baseline − target) / years
assumptions: Linear absolute reduction path without rebound or population adjustment.

methods.title: Renewable electricity share
methods.equation: Renewable share = renewable electricity / total electricity
assumptions: Renewable and total electricity use the same accounting period.

methods.title: Fuel-to-electric electrification emissions savings
methods.equation: Savings = fuel energy × fuel factor − electric energy × grid factor
assumptions: Energy quantities represent equivalent delivered services.

methods.title: Transport modal-shift emissions savings
methods.equation: Savings = shifted trips × distance × factor difference
assumptions: Trips are genuinely shifted rather than newly induced; factors use compatible occupancy assumptions.

methods.title: Building-retrofit emissions savings
methods.equation: Savings = energy saved × emissions factor
assumptions: Saved energy and emissions factor refer to the same energy carrier and marginal or average basis.

methods.title: Carbon-budget exhaustion time
methods.equation: Years = remaining carbon budget / annual emissions
assumptions: Annual emissions remain constant in this screening calculation.

methods.title: Population heat-exposure rate
methods.equation: Exposure = exposed population / total population
assumptions: Exposure threshold and population data refer to the same event or scenario.

methods.title: Cooling-degree-day increase
methods.equation: Increase = future CDD − baseline CDD
assumptions: Degree-day bases and time periods are consistent.

methods.title: Flood expected annual damage
methods.equation: EAD = probability × damage
assumptions: Single-event screening approximation; full EAD requires integrating the complete damage-probability curve.

methods.title: Distributed stormwater retention
methods.equation: Retained volume = rainfall × treated area × retention fraction
assumptions: Uniform storm depth and retention performance over the treated area.

methods.title: Drought supply-demand ratio
methods.equation: Ratio = reliable drought supply / drought demand
assumptions: Supply and demand use a common drought scenario and reliability definition.

methods.title: Wildfire-interface exposure rate
methods.equation: Exposure = exposed assets / total assets
assumptions: Exposure area and asset inventory use compatible spatial definitions.

methods.title: Sea-level-rise population exposure
methods.equation: Exposure = population below elevation threshold / total population
assumptions: Elevation threshold, protection assumptions, and population year are explicitly documented.

methods.title: Adaptation benefit-cost ratio
methods.equation: BCR = present value of avoided loss / adaptation cost
assumptions: Avoided loss and adaptation cost use the same discounting and analysis period.

methods.title: Critical-service reliability
methods.equation: Reliability = delivered service / required service
assumptions: Delivered and required service use the same operating period and unit.

methods.title: Critical-system redundancy
methods.equation: Redundancy = backup capacity / required capacity
assumptions: Backup capacity is operationally independent and available during the stated event.

methods.title: Infrastructure reserve margin
methods.equation: Reserve margin = (capacity − peak demand) / peak demand
assumptions: Available capacity is deliverable under the stated operating condition.

methods.title: Customer outage-hours
methods.equation: Outage burden = affected customers × outage duration
assumptions: Affected-customer count and outage duration describe the same incident.

methods.title: Linear service recovery time to target
methods.equation: Time = total recovery duration × (target − initial)/(final − initial)
assumptions: Service recovers linearly between the stated initial and final conditions.

methods.title: Resilience-triangle service loss
methods.equation: Loss = duration × [1 − average service]
assumptions: Linear recovery trajectory.

methods.title: Backup-system autonomy
methods.equation: Autonomy = stored usable energy / critical load
assumptions: Critical load is approximately constant and storage availability is fully represented by usable energy.

methods.title: Infrastructure interdependency risk score
methods.equation: Risk = probability × consequence × dependency multiplier
assumptions: Probability, consequence, and dependency multiplier are explicitly documented screening inputs.

methods.title: Household energy burden
methods.equation: Energy burden = annual household energy cost / annual household income
assumptions: Costs and income use compatible household and annual definitions.

methods.title: Transit-access equity ratio
methods.equation: Equity ratio = priority-population access / citywide access
assumptions: Access fractions use the same threshold, travel mode, and opportunity definition.

methods.title: Green-space equity gap
methods.equation: Gap = citywide green space per capita − priority-area value
assumptions: Green-space inventories and population measures use comparable definitions.

methods.title: Weighted heat-vulnerability score
methods.equation: Score = weighted exposure + sensitivity + adaptive-capacity deficit
assumptions: Component scores use a common normalization and weights are explicit planning choices.

methods.title: Household resilience-upgrade burden
methods.equation: Burden = annualized resilience cost / household income
assumptions: Annualized costs and household income use compatible terms.

methods.title: Basic-service deprivation rate
methods.equation: Deprivation = population lacking service / total population
assumptions: Service definition and population data use the same geographic boundary.

methods.title: Accessibility disparity index
methods.equation: Disparity = (high-access − low-access) / citywide access
assumptions: Opportunity counts use the same threshold, mode, and destination inventory.

methods.title: Equitable resilience-investment priority
methods.equation: Priority = vulnerability × service gap × benefit potential
assumptions: Scores are transparent screening inputs and do not automate allocation decisions.

methods.title: Weighted sustainable-city score
methods.equation: Score = weighted environment + equity + economy + resilience
assumptions: Component scores use a common scale and weights represent an explicit governance choice.

methods.title: Localized SDG target-progress score
methods.equation: Progress = weighted achieved targets / weighted target total

inputs.label: Weight of achieved or on-track targets

assumptions: Target status and weights are documented and regularly reviewed.

methods.title: Policy implementation rate
methods.equation: Implementation = completed actions / planned actions
assumptions: Actions use a documented completion definition and reporting period.

methods.title: Sustainability portfolio net present value
methods.equation: NPV = annual benefit annuity − initial cost
assumptions: Annual net benefit and real discount rate remain constant.

methods.title: Emissions target gap
methods.equation: Gap = projected emissions − target emissions
assumptions: Projection and target use the same accounting boundary.

methods.title: Resilience target gap
methods.equation: Gap = target resilience score − projected score
assumptions: Target and projected scores use the same indicator framework.

methods.title: Climate-action co-benefit score
methods.equation: Co-benefit = weighted health-supporting + equity + economic + ecological benefits
assumptions: Scores are non-clinical planning indicators and weights are explicit scenario choices.

methods.title: Multi-scenario robustness score
methods.equation: Robustness = minimum scenario performance / target performance
assumptions: Scenario scores use the same performance framework and target.
